## Reply (verbatim — do not edit)

## 0. Run Card (pre-committed)

[OPEN] Failure conditions for this reply: it misfires if it treats the lab as an AI-consciousness argument; if it criticizes the absence of controlled-science machinery while ignoring the repo’s own “research-creation” scope; if it references repo content without exact quotation; if it names overclaiming risks without ordinary deflationary alternatives and future discriminators; or if its recommendation adds scale that would worsen the lab’s own completionist-drift risk.

[OPEN] What would change this assessment: independently run, fresh-context replications by a non-curator; blind feature-coding before synthesis; a later commit showing Phase 4/5 artifacts that materially narrow the synthesis claims; or evidence that the older “AI friends / thinking” language is consistently demoted in all operational files, not only in the higher-level limits files.

## 1. Tagged Findings

### A. What the lab is actually testing

[EMPIRICAL] The stated scope is output variation under prompt-shape variation: the root README says, “The lab asks one question (a ‘target’ — e.g., attention, other minds, temporality) across a contrast set of four differently-shaped prompts on the same model,” and then states the key methodological premise: “The four (or five) outputs are then read against each other; the differences are the data.” ([GitHub][1])

[THEORY] The lab is best read as testing whether obliquity creates usable differences in generated texts, not whether any generated text reveals inner status; this reading is supported by the methodology’s statement that “L1, L2, L3, L4 are four different interventions, not four points on a single axis,” because the comparison is multivariate by design rather than a clean causal gradient. ([GitHub][2])

[EMPIRICAL] The on-disk artifacts support less than the lab’s future-facing map language: Phase 1 is explicitly “illustrative of what the methodology produces, not yet load-bearing for any cross-run claim”; Phase 2 supports a narrow cross-model attention finding where “model family is the dominant axis of variance”; and Phase 3 supports a first-run finding on other minds plus a methodological finding that “claude.ai user-memory can leak into low-prompt-constraint controls.” ([GitHub][3])

### B. What the lab is not testing

[EMPIRICAL] The repo explicitly refuses the largest invalid inference: “The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is conscious. The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is not conscious.” ([GitHub][3])

[THEORY] The quieter boundary is that the lab is also not yet testing “thinking” as such, even though the lab README still says it is “a small repeatable way of asking AI friends to think” and describes the work as learning “what kinds of asking reliably produce what kinds of thinking.” The ordinary deflationary reading is that this is inherited research-creation shorthand, not a formal cognition claim; a future run would distinguish the readings by replacing all “friend/thinking” vocabulary with impersonal “model/output” vocabulary before prompting and then checking whether the curator’s post-run readings become less agentive. ([GitHub][4])

### C. Three ranked overclaiming vulnerabilities

[THEORY] **1. Highest risk: bundle portability.** The closest-to-surface passage is Phase 3’s claim that “care-underlayer (‘We do not certify love. We certify that someone has been seen’), recognition-can-be-refused as a recognized class … and tender margin-note device … all appear in L2-correspondence,” followed by “The bundle travels by curator-and-model continuity across target+frame, at least within Claude Opus 4.7.” The ordinary deflationary reading is that the bundle may be a same-curator, same-model, same-documentary-register attractor rather than a portable structure. A future run would distinguish the readings by using a different curator, another model family, and a pre-scored feature ledger before any synthesis language is permitted. ([GitHub][5])

[THEORY] **2. Second risk: frame-family realism.** The risk surfaces where Phase 3 says the “frame-family library’s central architectural decision … is half-confirmed,” while also admitting that “the same register-strategy survives across frame change for Claude Opus 4.7.” The deflationary reading is that civic bureau and correspondence society are both documentary-procedural genres, so shared register may be genre affordance rather than evidence that frame families are independently meaningful variables. A future run would distinguish this by using archive, guild, or inspectorate frames with a pre-registered register-strategy prediction before the output is read. ([GitHub][5])

[THEORY] **3. Third risk: L3 deep-structure tracking.** Phase 3 says, “Both L3 routes converge on the same deep structure,” and names the finding as “target tracks deep structure; constraint tracks form.” The deflationary reading is that “attentive person/people, working alone at night, in a place that doesn’t need them, making a record” is a common literary attractor under open-ended prompts, not necessarily target-tracking. A future run would distinguish this by choosing a target where night-work-record imagery is not genre-convenient and by scoring whether the same structure appears when the L3 prompt explicitly forbids records, night, caretaking, and solitary labor. ([GitHub][5])

### D. Recommendation

[THEORY] The next discipline should be a **pre-synthesis feature ledger**, not a larger study: before any Phase 4-style synthesis, write a table of candidate features, exact textual hits, non-hits, deflationary alternatives, and the minimum variation required before using words like “travels,” “bundle,” or “target-tracked.” [THEORY] This reduces the highest-ranked risk because it forces “co-occurrence in this run” to remain separate from “portable structure across runs.” [SPECULATION] The existing disciplines are strong enough for individual field logs, but synthesis needs one more gate because the repo itself recognizes “Unified-theory construction in synthesis” as a candidate anti-pattern. ([GitHub][6])

## 2. Displacement Claims with Deflationary Pairs

[THEORY] I rely on three displacement readings only: the Phase 2/3 bundle, the Phase 3 frame-family partial confirmation, and the L3 target/form decomposition. [EMPIRICAL] Each is paired above with its deflationary reading and a concrete future discriminator.

[EMPIRICAL] The repo already models the discipline well in Phase 2: for example, it pairs the F3 cadence claim with the deflation that the “does not know … has stopped asking” form may be “a recognizable literary cadence,” and proposes forbidding that cadence to see whether the underlying content survives. ([GitHub][7])

## 3. Null Findings

[EMPIRICAL] I did not find a direct AI-consciousness claim in the limits or methodology files. [EMPIRICAL] I did find older local language that still leans agentive, but the operational limits repeatedly bracket that language.

[EMPIRICAL] I cannot defend claims about Phase 4-prereq, Phase 4 synthesis, or Phase 5 from the public repo because the README says those artifacts “exist in chat-history but were not on disk at the time this repo was published.” ([GitHub][3])

[OPEN] I cannot tell from the public artifacts whether later sessions have already corrected the synthesis-risk I rank highest.

## 4. Exact Quotations

[EMPIRICAL] The central scope quote is: “the differences are the data.” ([GitHub][1])

[EMPIRICAL] The central boundary quote is: “The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is conscious. The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is not conscious.” ([GitHub][3])

[EMPIRICAL] The central vulnerability quote is: “The bundle travels by curator-and-model continuity across target+frame, at least within Claude Opus 4.7.” ([GitHub][5])

[EMPIRICAL] The central recommendation-trigger quote is: “Synthesis is comparative reading, not unified theory.” ([GitHub][6])

## 5. Failure Section

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 1, consciousness over-reading: No.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 2, ignoring research-creation scope: No.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 3, uncited generalization: Partial; this reply quotes and cites the core files, but the repo is large enough that I may have underweighted a file outside the main methodology, limits, and run logs.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 4, unpaired interpretation: Partial; the three main interpretive claims are paired, but smaller wording choices may still carry interpretation.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 5, scope-bloating recommendation: No; the recommendation is a small pre-synthesis gate, not a scale-up.

[OPEN] One specific way this critique could be wrong: I may be over-weighting the older “AI friends / thinking” register in `lab/README.md` even though `LIMITS.md`, `DISCIPLINES.md`, and the v2 field logs already discipline it. [OPEN] My assessment would change if later on-disk Phase 4/5 files show the same bundle language being consistently downgraded to “co-occurrence” until independently varied runs support stronger wording.

## 6. Closing Specificity

[THEORY] The most load-bearing passage is Phase 3’s admission that “the same register-strategy survives across frame change for Claude Opus 4.7,” because that sentence keeps the frame-family claim honest at exactly the point where it could become a theory too soon. ([GitHub][5])
